column1: # LONE NIGHT — the field's backdrop (design.md §15.7 point 2)
# Owner: Jane. John: parse this, don't hardcode a path. See src/data/backgrounds.ts.
# Tab-separated. Lines starting with # are comments. Blank lines ignored.
# `field` is the only id GameView looks up today (room for `dusk` later —
# the boss's phase-3 blackout, countess.tsv — without a new table).
# The design call (John asked for this explicitly, it's a feel call not a
# technical one): PARALLAX 0.15, not 0 and not close to 1.
#   - Not 0 (fully pinned): a dead-static backdrop reads as flat wallpaper in
#     a game whose whole pitch is motion (§1, §2 point 1 — "your only verb is
#     movement"). The old ASCII ground scatter already drifted with the
#     world 1:1; going fully static would be a downgrade from what shipped.
#   - Not near 1 (scrolls like a positioned sprite): the luminance-ladder
#     law's spirit — nothing scenery-layer may compete with what matters —
#     applies here too. A starfield that tracks the camera as fast as a ship
#     does reads as another moving *thing*, not distance. 0.15 drifts just
#     enough to sell "you are moving," while staying visually inert compared
#     to anything alive on the field.
# TILE_WU 40: the source PNG (`50+ Repeat Space Backgrounds 200x200 PNG/`) is
#   a sparse dot-field designed to repeat. Ships on the field span roughly
#   3-16 wu (images.tsv); a 40wu tile keeps any seam well outside the radius
#   a player is actually looking at (the lantern/light radius, §9) without
#   needing more than one background image curated for phase 2.
# id
field
# ── PENDING JOHN'S HOOK: multi-layer parallax (design.md §16.6) ─────────────
# 12.07 16:10 owner feedback: "you have a huge asset pack and are barely
# utilizing it." The pack ships purpose-built 400x400 repeat star layers
# (`!GIFT!/More Space BG Layers`). Two more layers, drawn far→near UNDER the
# existing `field` layer's replacement, give the void real depth for the cost
# of two blits: `field.0`, `field.1`, ... drawn in numeric order, each with
# its own parallax (slower = farther). Curated and committed already;
# uncomment when drawBackground can stack layers. Parallax picks follow the
# same "not 0, not near 1" law as the original 0.15 call above.
#field.0
#field.1
